items.title: Trump agrees to 'double-sided ceasefire' less than 90 minutes before deadline

children.content: Donald Trump says he has agreed to suspend US attacks on Iran for two weeks as part of a "double-sided ceasefire". Iran is claiming victory and says it will stop its attacks if the US does.

image.altText: Donald Trump stands by a US flag in front of a White House image on a blue wall.

items.title: PM welcomes ceasefire but says Trump's language wasn't 'appropriate'

children.content: Anthony Albanese has described the two-week US-Iran ceasefire as positive news but says Donald Trump's rhetoric overnight wasn't appropriate. Follow live.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese speaks at the National Press Club.

items.title: ASX rallies more than 2pc as oil falls back below $US100 on Iran deal

children.content: The US president announced a two-week ceasefire, as the clock counted down to his Iran deadline. The ASX has jumped on the news, while oil futures have plunged about 15pc. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: 3D printed oil barrels and rising stock graph

items.title: Cost of filling best-selling car up by $100 since start of Iran war

children.content: The ABC analyses how the cost of a full tank in Australia's most-purchased cars of 2025 has changed since the beginning of the Iran war.

image.altText: A row of ford utes outside a dealership.

items.title: Ben Roberts-Smith to remain in custody on war crimes charges

children.content: War veteran Ben Roberts-Smith will remain in custody after his lawyers made no application for bail and a court adjourned his criminal case until June.

articles.title: Ben Roberts-Smith's fight to clear his name just got more perilous

children.content: During his years-long battle to clear his name, a separate joint investigation stretching 9,000 kilometres was discreetly transpiring.

image.altText: Ben Roberts Smith in a blue suit, white shirt, outside court on a sunny day.

items.title: Vance calls Trump as US rallies for Viktor Orbán

children.content: US President Donald Trump has sent his vice-president to Hungary in a bid to salvage the political future of far-right leader Viktor Orbán, who is flailing in the polls days before a closely watched election.

image.altText: Viktor Orbán and JD Vance wave at a political rally in Hungary

items.title: Farmers angered by plans to turn coal mine into lake bigger than Sydney Harbour

children.content: There are plans to turn the former Hazelwood coal mine in Victoria into a lake bigger than Sydney Harbour, but farmers and environmentalists are worried about the impact on nearby waterways.

children.content: Liam Parry, 33, was arrested and charged last month for reciting the banned phrase "from the river to the sea."

items.title: Smallest town in Australia for sale as two residents decide to sell roadhouse

children.content: Cooladdi, nine hours west of Brisbane, dwindled in population when its railway station closed, but it continues to attract road-trippers and graziers. Its owners run local services including a shop, motel and mail service.

items.title: 'A worker is going to be stabbed': Union airs hospital safety concerns

children.content: NT Health has rejected calls for a security review at Royal Darwin Hospital's busy emergency department amid threats against staff, with staff concerned "a worker is going to be stabbed".

items.title: Where cattle once roamed, conservation now grows — and so do concerns

children.content: Hailed as a win for the environment, $200 million has been spent purchasing new national parks in outback NSW — but not everyone is celebrating.

items.title: Water storage feud heats up as frustrated farmers take on corporate giants

items.title: Worksafe investigates fatal crash on WA mine access road

children.content: A 38-year-old man has died in a crash on an access road to the King of the Hills mine site, near Leonora.

image.altText: The view from a lookout at a gold mine showing the entrance to an underground portal.

items.title: Court sets aside convictions of parents for starving daughter, grants bail

children.content: A Perth man and woman who were found guilty in 2024 of starving their daughter, who weighed about 28 kilograms when she was admitted to hospital aged nearly 17, have their convictions set aside on appeal.

image.altText: A digitally produced image featuring a man and a woman covering their heads with jackets, and a slightly built girl.

items.title: Tourists urged not to cancel plans as Cyclone Maila intensifies

children.content: Severe Tropical Cyclone Maila is now a category five system and is expected to cross into Far North Queensland on Sunday.

image.altText: computer image of cyclone off queensland coast

items.title: Why this photo shoot made rugby star Mel Kawa feel like a fraud

children.content: Recently retired rugby union player Mel Kawa regularly broke down in tears, grappling with her love of the game and the $2,000 she was paid to play it.

items.title: Boards make decisions affecting the nation but they don't reflect modern society

children.content: There's a push to get younger people, women and more diverse community members on the boards making the big decisions in Australian life.

items.title: Matt Canavan looks to stars for 'Australia on steroids' plan

children.content: A domestic space industry, a baby boom, new dams and more factories are on Nationals leader Matt Canavan's to-do list to deliver what he describes as an Australian economic revolution.

items.title: Calls to force NDIS providers to register as Labor eyes savings

children.content: Stricter registration rules would weed out low-quality NDIS providers while delivering better services and budget savings, major operators say.

children.content: Babies, some as young as three months, were allegedly sold to people in Singapore and across Indonesia.

children.content: Hundreds of legal, health and children's organisations are calling on Anthony Albanese to convene an emergency summit to address the over-incarceration of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander children.

items.title: Growing frustration in real estate industry over Qld seller disclosure laws

children.content: Real estate experts say Queensland's seller disclosure laws, which were introduced in August, are fuelling 'gazumping' and leading to unnecessary costs.

items.title: Darwin midwife's $1.1 million government contract under review

children.content: Darwin midwife Claire Marks was under investigation by the nursing and midwifery watchdog when she was handed a $1.1 million contract by the NT government, which is now reviewing the matter, as doctors continue to raise concerns, and the service brings on new staff.

image.altText: A woman in a brown dress looks at the camera.

items.title: Claims wild-caught Australian seafood will soon 'only be afforded by the rich'

children.content: Australian seafood prices are likely to rise due to fuel supply issues, with some warning that wild-caught seafood could become unaffordable for most.

image.altText: Prawns at a seafood market

items.title: Varroa mite 'in all corners' of ACT as more than 1,000 beehives destroyed

children.content: Experts say half of the ACT's 6,000 beehives have been impacted by the fast-spreading parasite varroa mite with more than 1,000 destroyed.

image.altText: A beekeeper lifts a beehive from its structure.

items.title: Duke to retire from Socceroos after World Cup bid

children.content: Mitch Duke will retire from international football this year, but is still hopeful of being part of Australia's FIFA World Cup squad.

items.title: Blues recruit's striking ban upheld by AFL tribunal

children.content: Carlton will be without Will Hayward for a high-stakes clash against Adelaide after failing to downgrade his one-match ban for striking.

items.title: 'They blocked menstrual pads': Inside Myanmar's conflict zone, where monasteries are bombed

children.content: Women who had already lost their homes to last year's earthquake are now also cut off from essentials like sanitary pads and aid.
